mainEntity.name: What advantages do AI-based calculations offer compared to manual processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: AI-driven features offer several key advantages: Time savings – Automated analyses reduce manual effort and significantly speed up calculations. Scalability – Even large or complex datasets can be processed consistently and quickly. Reduced risk of error – Standardized processes minimize input and transmission errors. Transparency – Results are accompanied by confidence scores, and all estimates or uncertainties are clearly indicated.

mainEntity.name: Can the AI results be verified or adjusted?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. All results generated by the AI are presented transparently. Additionally, a Confidence Score is displayed, which assesses the certainty of the recognition or assignment, along with a brief explanation ('Reasoning'). You can review, validate, and manually adjust the results at any time – the control always remains with you.

mainEntity.name: How does the AI deal with missing or incomplete data?

acceptedAnswer.text: Our AI can work with both complete and incomplete information. If certain data is missing, the AI generates estimates based on the available information. Each estimate comes with a confidence score that indicates the reliability of the assumption, as well as a brief explanation. Whenever you provide primary data on materials, quantities, or emission values, these take precedence. Automatically generated estimates can be replaced at any time with your own, more accurate inputs.

mainEntity.name: Are the results checked or can the AI make mistakes?

acceptedAnswer.text: We continuously improve our AI and ensure that it meets the highest quality standards as well as established calculation norms. However, as with any automated process, errors can occur. Therefore, we recommend checking the results and providing us with feedback in case of discrepancies or suggestions for improvement.

mainEntity.name: Will my data be used to train the AI?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Your data will not be used for training AI models. They are solely for the calculation you requested and will not be further processed after completion.

mainEntity.name: How secure is my data when using AI?

acceptedAnswer.text: The processing takes place exclusively on our own servers in Germany. All data is transmitted and stored end-to-end encrypted, is fully GDPR-compliant, and is not shared with third parties.

mainEntity.name: Will my data be automatically sent to the AI?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Data is only transmitted to the AI when you actively perform one of the following AI-supported functions: 1. Start of a Scope 3.1 / 3.2 emissions calculation. 2. Creation of a PCF calculation. Without your explicit action, no data transmission or automatic processing takes place.

mainEntity.name: Why is ChatGPT or Copilot not enough to calculate a PCF?

acceptedAnswer.text: Generative AI tools like ChatGPT or Copilot can research information, structure texts, or make rough assumptions. However, they are not sufficient for a reliable product carbon footprint (PCF) calculation. A verifiable PCF requires clearly defined system boundaries according to ISO 14067 and ISO 14040/44, consistent application of emission factors from integrated, versioned databases, reproducible calculation logic instead of one-time text output, transparent documentation of all assumptions, and comparability across products, time periods, and locations. Generative AI works probabilistically. It generates answers based on probabilities - not based on a structured, rule-based accounting system. There are no firmly implemented system boundaries, no binding methodology, and no guaranteed reproducibility of the same calculation. Global Changer combines AI-supported automation with fixed methodological standards, integrated emission factors, rule-based logic, and traceable structure. This results in scalable, audit-capable PCFs - not just text-based approximations.
